subject: Human Resources
title: Maslow and the Hierarchy of Needs
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Maslow and the Hierarchy of Needs.

hook.text: In 1943, Abraham Maslow proposed that humans have five levels of needs—and work satisfies more than just money.

buildup.text: Maslow's hierarchy: physiological, safety, belonging, esteem, and self-actualization—stacked like a pyramid.

discovery.text: His insight: once basic needs are met, people crave purpose, recognition, and growth—not just bigger paychecks.

twist.text: This framework revolutionized HR. Employee motivation became about the whole person, not just their output.

climax.text: Maslow gave HR the language to argue for engagement, culture, and development—not just compensation.

punchline.text: People need meaning, not just money.

quiz.question: What is at the top of Maslow's hierarchy?
